-- =============================================
-- Author:      <Author, , Name>
-- Create Date: <Create Date, , >
-- Description: Returns a list of cautions and alerts for one monitor and month
-- =============================================
CREATE PROCEDURE [dbo].[MonitorStatusForMonth]
(
     @MonitorId [uniqueidentifier] , 
     @Year		[int]  ,
     @Month		[int]  ,
	 @StartDate [datetime],
	 @EndDate   [datetime]
 )
AS
BEGIN
 
SELECT Min([AlertType]) as status, Day([NotificationTime])  as day 
  FROM  [dbo].[Notifications]
  where MonitorId = @MonitorId
  and MONTH([NotificationTime]) = @Month 
  AND YEAR([NotificationTime]) = @Year
  AND [NotificationTime] >= @StartDate
  AND [NotificationTime] <= @EndDate
  and AlertType <2 --Exclude offline and battery
  Group by Day([NotificationTime]) 
END

-- =============================================
-- Author:      <Author, , Name>
-- Create Date: <Create Date, , >
-- Description: <Description, , >
-- =============================================
CREATE PROCEDURE [dbo].[MonitorStatusTimeCheck]
(
     @MonitorId [uniqueidentifier]  
 )
AS
BEGIN
 
SET NOCOUNT ON

Declare @MonitorDate as datetime2(7);
Declare @LastApiDate as datetime2(7);
Declare @TypeMonitor as int;

SELECT TOP 1  @MonitorDate = CASE   
		  WHEN  [LastDataTime1Min] is not null  THEN [LastDataTime1Min]  
		  WHEN  LastDataTime15Min is not null  THEN LastDataTime15Min  
		  WHEN  LastDataTime1Hour is not null  THEN LastDataTime1Hour  
		  ELSE  ISNULL(LastDataTime24Hour ,'1970-01-01')
		END,
		@TypeMonitor =[TypeOfMonitor] 
FROM [dbo].[MonitorsList] WITH (NOLOCK)
where ID= @MonitorId 

-- This is taking a long time and even times out now and again.. Not used so removing it!!
--SET @LastApiDate = CASE @TypeMonitor
--     WHEN 0 THEN (SELECT TOP (1) [SampleTime]  FROM [dbo].[MyAtmDustLevels] order by SampleTime desc)
--	 WHEN 1 THEN (SELECT TOP (1) [SampleTime]  FROM [dbo].[OmnidotsPeakLevels] order by SampleTime desc)
--	 ELSE  (SELECT TOP (1) [SampleTime]  FROM [dbo].[AirQNoiseLevels] order by SampleTime desc)
--END 

Select @MonitorDate as MonitorDate, @MonitorDate as LastApiDate, GETUTCDATE() as UtcDate
END

-- =============================================
-- Author:      <Author, , Name>
-- Create Date: <Create Date, , >
-- Description: Returns a list of cautions and alerts for one monitor and month
-- =============================================
CREATE PROCEDURE [dbo].[PeakRecordBreachAndAlerts]
(
     @Date [datetime]
 )
AS
BEGIN
 
 SELECT [P].[SerialId], [M].[FleetNr], M.[Id] as 'Monitor Id', [P].[SampleTime], [N].[Id]  as 'Notification Id', [N].[NotificationTime], P.XVtop, P.YVtop, P.ZVtop
  FROM [dbo].[OmnidotsPeakLevels] P
  INNER JOIN [dbo].[MonitorsList] M ON [M].SerialId = P.SerialId and M.TypeOfMonitor=2
  LEFT Join [dbo].Notifications N ON [M].[Id] = [N].[MonitorId] AND N.[NotificationTime] = SampleTime and [AlertType] in (0,1)
  WHERE Cast([SampleTime] as date) = Cast(@Date as date)
  AND (XVtop > 7  OR [YVtop] > 7 OR ZVtop > 7) 

  order by [P].[SerialId], SampleTime
END
